summary: Surah Al-Kahf contains lessons from four major stories: the People of the Cave, the owner of two gardens, Musa and Khidr, and Dhul-Qarnain. It teaches about faith, patience, humility, and the reality of this world versus the Hereafter. The surah warns against arrogance, pride in wealth, and following falsehood, while encouraging reliance on Allah, gratitude, and righteous deeds. It emphasizes the test of faith, the importance of seeking true knowledge, and Allah’s mercy and guidance.
